entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: The crop Glycine max (L.) Merr. studied for N2 fixation and yield responses.

entities.name: N2 FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Water-stressed environmental condition affecting plant growth.

entities.name: 15N METHODOLOGIES
entities.description: Techniques using the 15N isotope to quantify nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: UREIDES
entities.description: Nitrogen-rich compounds whose relative abundance is used to estimate N2 fixation.

entities.name: RAU TECHNIQUE
entities.description: The relative abundance of ureides technique used for estimating N2 fixation.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with adequate water supply.

entities.name: N ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The process and rate of nitrogen buildup in plant tissues.

entities.name: N FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of inorganic nitrogen to plants.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by the soybean plant.

entities.name: INORGANIC N
entities.description: Nitrogen in a form that is directly available for plant uptake and assimilation.

entities.name: 15N-DILUTION
entities.description: A specific isotope dilution method used to estimate N2 fixation.
